beginner.question: What are CSS logical properties?
beginner.answer: CSS logical properties provide equivalent physical properties (like left, right, top, bottom) that adapt to the writing mode and direction of the document.

beginner.question: What is inset-inline?
beginner.answer: inset-inline is the logical equivalent of left/right positioning, controlling start and end positions in the inline direction.

beginner.question: What is margin-block?
beginner.answer: margin-block is the logical equivalent of margin-top and margin-bottom, applying margins in the block direction (vertical in horizontal writing).

beginner.question: What is border-inline?
beginner.answer: border-inline is the shorthand for border-inline-start and border-inline-end, applying borders along the inline axis (horizontal in LTR).

beginner.question: What are writing modes in CSS?
beginner.answer: Writing modes (writing-mode property) define whether text flows horizontally (horizontal-tb) or vertically (vertical-rl, vertical-lr).

beginner.question: How do logical properties map in a left-to-right layout?
beginner.answer: inline-start = left, inline-end = right, block-start = top, block-end = bottom in horizontal LTR writing mode.

beginner.question: What is the inline-start property?
beginner.answer: inline-start is the logical equivalent of left in LTR or right in RTL, depending on the direction and writing mode.

beginner.question: What does block-size mean?
beginner.answer: block-size is the logical equivalent of height; it changes with writing mode to maintain the block dimension regardless of text direction.

beginner.question: What does inline-size mean?
beginner.answer: inline-size is the logical equivalent of width; it represents the dimension along the inline axis which changes with writing mode.

beginner.question: Why use logical properties instead of physical ones?
beginner.answer: Logical properties create layouts that automatically adapt to different writing modes and directions, improving internationalization support.

intermediate.question: How do logical properties behave in a right-to-left (RTL) layout?
intermediate.answer: inline-start maps to right and inline-end maps to left automatically, maintaining proper spacing when direction changes.

intermediate.question: What is the difference between inset-inline and inset-block?
intermediate.answer: inset-inline controls start/end along the inline axis (horizontal LTR); inset-block controls start/end along the block axis (vertical).

intermediate.question: How do you set padding on only the inline-start side using logical properties?
intermediate.answer: Use padding-inline-start: value; which targets the start edge of the inline axis regardless of writing mode.

intermediate.question: What is border-inline-start vs border-left?
intermediate.answer: border-inline-start maps to the logical start edge; in LTR it equals border-left, but in RTL it equals border-right automatically.

intermediate.question: How do logical properties work with vertical writing modes?
intermediate.answer: In vertical writing, block becomes horizontal and inline becomes vertical; logical properties swap dimensions accordingly.

intermediate.question: What are the logical properties for margin, padding, and border?
intermediate.answer: Each has -inline, -block, -inline-start/end, -block-start/end variants, plus shorthands like margin-block, padding-inline.

intermediate.question: How do you center an element using logical properties?
intermediate.answer: Use margin-inline: auto; to center horizontally in any writing mode, or place-content center with logical grid/flex properties.

intermediate.question: What is the text-align equivalent for logical direction?
intermediate.answer: text-align: start and text-align: end are logical equivalents of left and right, respecting writing direction.

intermediate.question: How do logical properties work in CSS Grid and Flexbox?
intermediate.answer: Grid and Flexbox use logical properties by default (start, end, etc.); physical directions like left/right can override but may break in RTL.

intermediate.question: Can you mix physical and logical properties in the same rule?
intermediate.answer: Yes, but it can lead to unexpected results; mixing is generally discouraged for consistency.

advanced.question: How do logical properties interact with the position property?
advanced.answer: Use inset-inline and inset-block for absolute/fixed positioning instead of left/right/top/bottom for direction-aware positioning.

advanced.question: How do you set a logical border-radius corner?
advanced.answer: Use border-start-start-radius, border-start-end-radius, etc., which adapt to the writing direction.

advanced.question: How do logical properties affect CSS animations and transitions?
advanced.answer: Animate logical properties like margin-inline-start; the animation works correctly regardless of the document's writing direction.

advanced.question: How do you use logical properties with the contain property?
advanced.answer: contain: inline-size uses the logical inline dimension; it contains the element based on writing mode, not physical width.

advanced.question: How do logical properties interact with scroll-snap?
advanced.answer: Use scroll-snap-align: start and end which are logical; they adapt to writing direction automatically for consistent snapping.

advanced.question: What is the logical property for the overflow property?
advanced.answer: Use overflow-inline and overflow-block instead of overflow-x and overflow-y for direction-aware overflow control.

advanced.question: How do logical properties work with CSS transforms?
advanced.answer: Transform functions like translate are physical by default; use logical custom properties or calculate physical values from logical inputs.

advanced.question: How do logical properties affect web accessibility?
advanced.answer: Logical properties improve accessibility for international users by automatically adapting to their language's writing direction.

advanced.question: How do you create a direction-aware card component using logical properties?
advanced.answer: Use padding-inline, margin-block, border-inline-start, and inset-inline for all spacing; the card adapts to any writing mode automatically.

advanced.question: How do browser DevTools display logical properties?
advanced.answer: DevTools compute logical properties to physical equivalents in the computed styles panel, showing the resolved physical values.
